title: Race archetype priors for undercut vs overcut strength
summary: Public F1 strategy sources consistently separate high-degradation/undercut tracks from low-degradation or hard-warmup/overcut tracks.
rationale: Public F1 strategy sources consistently separate high-degradation/undercut tracks from low-degradation or hard-warmup/overcut tracks. The current pipeline uses race IDs but not explicit strategy archetypes, so mapping each circuit to degradation severity, warm-up sensitivity, and overtaking difficulty could help the model generalize beyond raw race labels.
implementation_hint: Create a small hand-built lookup by `Race` with 3-6 ordinal priors: degradation severity, warm-up difficulty, overtake difficulty, undercut strength, overcut viability. Interact these with `Compound`, `TyreLife`, `LapTime_Delta`, and `RaceProgress`; compare against plain race categorical in grouped CV.
expected_effect: Better separation of pit windows across tracks with similar tyre behavior, especially when race identity is sparse or shifted between years.
risk: Manual priors can be noisy or subjective; gains may vanish if race label already captures the same signal.
